info.title: MCP Hub API
info.description:
MCP Hub API provides a unified wrapper for discovering MCP servers, creating and managing connections, inspecting capabilities, invoking tools, reading resources, retrieving prompts, streaming events, and handling user/session authentication.

This contract is designed for both browser-based app sessions and programmatic clients, with support for cookie-based sessions and bearer-token authentication.

servers.description: Base path prefix for all API routes.

tags.description: MCP server contexts and models management

tags.description: Access to related tools integrated within MCP Hub

tags.name: runtime
tags.description: Per-connection MCP runtime operations such as initialize, tool invocation, and cancellation

get.description: Fetches prompt templates directly from the connected MCP server.

post.description: Requests cancellation for an active MCP request.

get.description: Returns whether the remote MCP server connection is authenticated and what auth type is active.

post.summary: Start auth flow for a connection
post.description: Bootstraps remote authentication for MCP servers that require browser or token-based authorization.

User.description: A user registered in the system

url.description: Base URL of the MCP server

description.example: Primary MCP server for region A

schema.description: JSON schema or metadata describing the model structure

url.description: URL or endpoint to access the tool

content.example: Translate the following text to French:

RequestDetails.description: Details and event history of a request

events.description: List of events related to the request

command.description: Executable used to launch the MCP server process.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the child process.

cwd.description: Working directory for the child process.

url.description: HTTP endpoint for the MCP server transport.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers sent with transport requests.

sessionId.description: Optional session identifier returned by the remote transport.

TransportOptionsStreamableHttp.description: Transport options for streamable HTTP MCP servers.

clientName.description: Human-readable name of the MCP Hub client.

clientVersion.description: Semantic version of the MCP Hub client.

example.instructions: Use resources for file reads and tools for write operations.

arguments.description: Arguments passed to the target tool.

uri.description: Resource URI exposed by the MCP server.

requestId.description: Identifier of the in-flight request to cancel.

callbackUrl.description: Client callback URL for completing auth.
